name: **Other AI email clients** (Canary Mail, Shortwave, Spike, etc.)
reason_not_approved: Not approved; admin-level permissions risk

name: **Other AI meeting bots** (Otter.ai, Notion AI, Sembly, etc.)
reason_not_approved: Admin-level meeting permissions; not approved
notes: Use Zoom AI Companion or M365 Copilot in Teams instead

name: Anthropic Claude (public cloud service)
reason_not_approved: Did not pass required reviews; not approved for Stanford/SLAC data
notes: Claude models available via SLAC AI Accelerator and Stanford AI Playground

name: ChatGPT (OpenAI – public)
reason_not_approved: Did not pass required reviews; not approved for Stanford/SLAC data
notes: Similar models available via Stanford AI Playground

name: Claude Code (public/direct)
reason_not_approved: Not approved for Stanford data as a standalone tool
notes: Use via SLAC AI Accelerator API hub (SLAC) or AI API Gateway (Stanford)

name: Claude in Excel
reason_not_approved: Under new review; not currently approved

name: Cursor
reason_not_approved: Not under review; not approved for Stanford data

name: DeepSeek (public)
reason_not_approved: Not currently under review; not approved for Stanford/SLAC data
notes: DeepSeek models available via Stanford AI Playground

name: Fireflies.ai
reason_not_approved: Admin-level meeting permissions; not approved
notes: Use Zoom AI Companion or M365 Copilot in Teams instead

name: Google Drive M365 Copilot connector
reason_not_approved: Under review; not currently approved

name: Google Gemini (unauthenticated / personal account)
reason_not_approved: Not approved for Stanford data without Stanford account login
notes: Use via [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com) with Stanford account instead

name: Google Gemini for Workspace
reason_not_approved: Cannot be purchased due to Google licensing limitations
notes: UIT is working with Google on a future solution

name: Google NotebookLM Plus
reason_not_approved: Cannot be purchased due to Google licensing limitations

name: Grammarly (non-Education versions)
reason_not_approved: Vendor does not support required service agreements for non-Education versions
notes: Use Grammarly Education with TSA+BAA only

name: Grok
reason_not_approved: Not approved for SLAC use
notes: Listed as prohibited by SLAC IT

name: Microsoft Copilot (unauthenticated)
reason_not_approved: Not approved for Stanford data; does not require SUNet ID sign-in

name: OpenClaw / Clawdbot / Moltbot
reason_not_approved: Not under review; not approved

name: Perplexity
reason_not_approved: Vendor does not support required service agreements

name: Read.ai
reason_not_approved: Admin-level meeting permissions; not approved
notes: Use Zoom AI Companion or M365 Copilot in Teams instead

name: Serper AI
reason_not_approved: Vendor does not support required service agreements

name: Superhuman
reason_not_approved: Did not pass required reviews; no required service agreements
notes: Use Microsoft 365 Copilot for email AI instead

name: Zapier
reason_not_approved: Not HIPAA-compliant; no enterprise license candidate

name: AWS Bedrock (SLAC)
description: Fully managed AWS service for building and scaling generative AI and agentic applications using foundation models from AWS, Anthropic, Meta, Mistral, and more. Provides unified APIs, evaluation tooling, and managed orchestration.
approved_for_text: Within SLAC-managed cloud environments; Low & Moderate-Risk data
not_approved_for_text: Use outside SLAC-managed cloud; High-Risk/CUI without separately authorized system

name: Claude Code (via SLAC AI Accelerator API)
description: Agentic AI coding tool that runs in the terminal to read, edit, and debug code across files. Understands project context, executes bash commands, runs tests, and manages Git workflows. At SLAC, must be connected to the SLAC AI Accelerator API hub (not the public Anthropic cloud service).
approved_for_text: Low & Moderate-Risk data when connected to SLAC API hub
not_approved_for_text: Direct use of public Anthropic/Claude cloud service; autonomous commits/merges without human review

name: GitHub Copilot (SLAC)
description: AI coding assistant integrated in IDEs (VS Code, etc.) that provides real-time code completions, transforms comments into code, and navigates unfamiliar codebases.
approved_for_text: SLAC-managed repositories only, with SLAC-provided license
not_approved_for_text: Non-SLAC-managed repos; autonomous PR merges or deployments

name: Google Cloud Platform / Vertex AI (SLAC – coming soon)
description: GCP architecture mirroring SLAC's AWS infrastructure, providing access to Gemini models, co-Scientist, and Jules. Currently in procurement.
not_approved_for_text: Not yet available

name: Joulix AI Application Suite
description: Google-powered, DOE-hosted AI tools enriched with DOE data to enhance productivity and foster innovation toward DOE's mission.
not_approved_for_text: Non-DOE use cases

name: Legion Intelligence (Developer Platform)
description: Commercially supported development and workflow hosting platform for complex operational use cases, offering vendor-supported agent orchestration, development, and hosting. Geared toward teams with less internal support.
not_approved_for_text: Not yet specified

name: Legion Suite
description: Enterprise AI search and workflow platform that breaks down data silos across SLAC systems (SharePoint, ServiceNow, Confluence, etc.). Features AI search, cited Q&A with hallucination detection, automated workflows, and permission-aware access. Data stays within SLAC's environment; supports on-premises, air-gapped, or cloud deployment with any LLM.
approved_for_text: Low & Moderate-Risk SLAC data; internal productivity and search
not_approved_for_text: Not specified for High-Risk/CUI data without separate review

name: Local-only AI Models
description: AI models run entirely on SLAC-authorized systems with no network calls to external services. Generally permitted without prior approval for public data.
approved_for_text: Low-Risk data on any SLAC-authorized system; CUI/Sensitive data if the underlying system is authorized for that classification
not_approved_for_text: Network-aware local models with non-public data (requires Cyber Security review first)

name: Microsoft 365 Copilot (SLAC)
description: AI assistant embedded in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams, and OneNote. Helps draft documents, analyze data, create presentations, summarize emails, recap meetings, and build personal productivity agents. SLAC provides training sessions (100/200/300 levels). Requires SLAC-provided license on SLAC-managed system.
approved_for_text: Approved on SLAC-managed systems with SLAC-provided license
not_approved_for_text: Non-SLAC-managed devices; use without an SLAC license

access_links.label: Request a full Copilot license (login required)

name: SLAC AI Accelerator
description: SLAC-governed platform giving access to curated AI models (Anthropic Claude, Meta Llama, OpenAI OSS, and more) for drafting, summarizing, coding, and working with SLAC datasets. Built on SLAC VPC; API access available within SLAC network. Pay-as-you-go ($500/month default API budget). Google Gemini models forthcoming.
approved_for_text: Low-Risk & Moderate-Risk SLAC data; scientific and operational use cases
not_approved_for_text: CUI or High-Risk data (requires separately authorized system); fully autonomous code commits/deployments

name: AWS Bedrock (Stanford)
description: Access to Amazon AI and third-party AI models via a Stanford AWS account (Cardinal Cloud). High-Risk/PHI use requires a DRA.
approved_for_text: Low-Risk · Moderate-Risk · High-Risk (non-PHI) · High-Risk (PHI) (Cardinal Cloud + Wiz compliance + DRA for High-Risk/PHI)
not_approved_for_text: Use outside Cardinal Cloud without Wiz compliance; High-Risk/PHI without a DRA

name: AWS Q (Stanford)
description: Managed generative AI–powered chat assistant available through a Stanford AWS account. Security review in progress; currently limited to Low-Risk data.
not_approved_for_text: Moderate-Risk and above pending security review completion

name: AWS SageMaker (Stanford)
description: Managed AWS platform for building, training, and deploying machine learning models. Available through a Stanford AWS account. High-Risk/PHI use requires a DRA.
approved_for_text: Low-Risk · Moderate-Risk · High-Risk (non-PHI) · High-Risk (PHI) (Cardinal Cloud + Wiz compliance + DRA)
not_approved_for_text: Use outside Cardinal Cloud without Wiz compliance; High-Risk/PHI without a DRA

name: Azure OpenAI (Stanford)
description: Access to Azure OpenAI and other third-party AI models via a Stanford Azure account (Cardinal Cloud). Commercially available with billing to a PTA. High-Risk/PHI use requires a Data Risk Assessment (DRA).
approved_for_text: Low-Risk · Moderate-Risk · High-Risk (non-PHI) · High-Risk (PHI) (Cardinal Cloud + Wiz compliance + DRA for High-Risk/PHI)
not_approved_for_text: Use outside Cardinal Cloud without Wiz compliance; High-Risk/PHI without a DRA

name: Google Vertex AI (Stanford)
description: Access to Google AI and third-party AI models via a Stanford GCP project (Cardinal Cloud). High-Risk/PHI use requires a DRA.
approved_for_text: Low-Risk · Moderate-Risk · High-Risk (non-PHI) · High-Risk (PHI) (Cardinal Cloud + Wiz compliance + DRA for High-Risk/PHI)
not_approved_for_text: Use outside Cardinal Cloud without Wiz compliance; High-Risk/PHI without a DRA

name: Microsoft 365 Copilot in Teams
description: M365 Copilot meeting features (transcripts, summaries, live questions) available with a Teams Meetings and/or Teams Premium license. Approved for High-Risk and PHI data within the Stanford M365 tenant.
not_approved_for_text: Use outside Stanford M365 tenant

name: Slack Huddle Notes
description: AI-powered note-taking built into Slack Huddles. Automatically transcribes and summarizes meetings including attendees, decisions, action items, and full transcript. Cannot include external guests.
approved_for_text: Low-Risk · Moderate-Risk · High-Risk (non-PHI) in private channels/DMs
not_approved_for_text: PHI; external guest huddles

name: Zoom AI Companion
description: Native Zoom features: meeting summaries, smart recording (chapters, highlights, action items), and in-meeting Q&A. Not enabled by default — hosts must turn on features in their Stanford Zoom web portal settings. As of Jan 26, 2026, participants must agree to an AI Companion Policy Disclaimer. High-Risk/PHI approval was rescinded in April 2025.
not_approved_for_text: High-Risk data (approval rescinded April 2025); PHI; third-party meeting bots

name: Authenticated Google Gemini Chat
description: Free, web-based conversational AI assistant (text, images, multimodal) powered by Google Gemini models. Must be signed in with Stanford Google account. Workspace integration (Gmail/Docs/Sheets/Slides) currently disabled for Stanford accounts due to licensing. Chats auto-deleted after 18 months.
not_approved_for_text: PHI; High-Risk data; PII; proprietary info; patent-related code

name: Google Docs / Slides Voice Typing
description: Built-in voice-to-text feature in Google Docs and Slides (microphone icon). Works in Chrome, Edge, and Safari. Approved when logged into Stanford Google account.
not_approved_for_text: N/A

name: Google NotebookLM
description: AI research and note-taking tool grounded in documents you upload (PDFs, research articles, notes). Uses Google Gemini to summarize, extract insights, answer questions, and generate content based only on your source material. Free via Stanford Google account.
not_approved_for_text: PHI; High-Risk data; proprietary info; PII

name: Grammarly (Education version only)
description: AI writing assistant for grammar checking, style improvement, tone suggestions, and AI-generated text. Only the Grammarly Education version with a signed Technical Service Agreement (TSA) and Business Associate Agreement (BAA) is approved.
not_approved_for_text: High-Risk data; PHI; use of any version other than Grammarly Education with TSA+BAA

name: Microsoft 365 Copilot (Stanford – paid)
description: Paid AI assistant deeply integrated into Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint, Teams (including meeting transcripts, summaries, live questions). Requires Stanford SUNet ID and per-user annual license. Approved for High-Risk and PHI data within the Stanford M365 tenant.
not_approved_for_text: Use outside the Stanford M365 tenant

name: Microsoft Copilot Chat (Authenticated)
description: Free AI chatbot running on Microsoft infrastructure. Chat-style interactions (text, image gen via DALL-E 3, summarization, code). Requires sign-in with Stanford SUNet ID. Data is not saved; Microsoft has no eyes-on access; not used to train models. Distinct from the paid M365 Copilot.
not_approved_for_text: PHI; High-Risk data; SSNs; financial account numbers

name: Slack AI Tools
description: AI features built into Stanford's Slack instance: channel/thread summaries, Slackbot AI assistant, message explanations, daily recaps, AI translations, automatic file summaries, canvas content generation, workflow automation, and Huddle Notes (transcription + summary). Data never leaves Slack-controlled infrastructure.
approved_for_text: Low-Risk · Moderate-Risk · High-Risk (non-PHI) in private channels/DMs only
not_approved_for_text: PHI data; third-party bots in meetings

name: Cline
description: AI coding assistant integrating with local dev environments (VS Code) to generate, modify, and execute code. Only approved when used with AI models through Stanford's AI API Gateway.
not_approved_for_text: Direct use with non-API-Gateway models; High-Risk data

name: GitHub Copilot (Stanford / Cardinal Cloud)
description: AI coding assistant that suggests code completions, functions, and documentation in IDEs. Requires a Cardinal Cloud Azure subscription (billed monthly at $19/user/month to a PTA). Only approved for Cardinal Cloud GitHub.
not_approved_for_text: Non–Cardinal Cloud GitHub repos; High-Risk data

name: Roo Code
description: Agentic coding tool for VS Code that generates code, refactors projects, and automates dev tasks. Only approved when connected to models via the AI API Gateway.
not_approved_for_text: Direct use with non-API-Gateway models; High-Risk data

name: AI API Gateway
description: Stanford's internal API access to all models available in the AI Playground. Intended for developers/researchers who want to build custom AI-powered solutions. Requires a valid Stanford PTA. Pay-as-you-go.
not_approved_for_text: High-Risk data; PHI; non-PTA holders

name: SHC-SoM Secure GPT
description: Stanford Medicine–hosted platform for testing multiple AI models in a secure environment. Available to select Stanford Medicine users only. Approved for sensitive and PHI data for Stanford Medicine users.
not_approved_for_text: Non–Stanford Medicine users

name: Stanford AI Playground
description: Stanford-hosted platform to test and compare multiple AI models (OpenAI GPT-4o/4.1/o1/o3-mini/gpt-5, Google Gemini 2.0/2.5-Pro, Anthropic Claude 3/4/4.5, DeepSeek R1/V3, Meta Llama 3.2/4) in one safe environment. Includes agents (image gen, financial search, Wolfram, Admin Guide, etc.), web search, file search, artifacts, model comparison, and memories. Managed by UIT as an ongoing pilot.
not_approved_for_text: High-Risk data; PHI; CUI; SLAC-internal/proprietary data
